0019:423F.label: restore_regs_and_return
0019:423F.comment: Epilogue trampoline for INT 21h handler. Restores SS from CS:[0x3DD7], stashes the return IP into ES:[0x05EE], then pops all saved registers (ax/bx/cx/dx/si/di/bp/ds) and jumps to the saved IP — returning to the original caller without touching any register.

0019:4275.label: get_caller_reg_frame
0019:4275.comment: Sets up DS:SI pointing at the caller's saved register frame on the interrupt stack. Loads SS from CS:[0x3DD7], then LDS SI from SS:[0x0584:0x0586] — the SS:SP pair saved at INT 21h entry. After return: DS = caller's SS, SI = caller's SP at interrupt time (bottom of the 9 pushed registers). Sub-handlers use [SI+offset] to write return values (AX=+0, BX=+2, CX=+4, DX=+6) and to modify the caller's FLAGS (+0x16, e.g. clearing carry for success).

0019:4256.label: build_register_frame
0019:4256.comment: Prologue helper. Restores SS from CS:[0x3DD7], stashes return IP in ES:[0x05EE], then pushes ds/bp/di/si/dx/cx/bx/ax to build a frame. Re-pushes the return IP and sets BP=SP so caller can address saved regs via [BP+offset]. Loads SS from [BP+0x14] (the word already on the stack before the call, i.e. caller's SS). Pair/inverse of restore_regs_and_return (0019:423F).

0250:0486.label: read_tick_count
0250:0486.comment: Reads the BIOS system timer tick count via INT 1Ah AH=0. Returns CX:DX = 32-bit tick count since midnight, AL = 1 if midnight has crossed since last read. Called by the tick-to-time conversion code at 0250:0435.

12E0:013F.label: bios_rtc_call
12E0:013F.comment: C-callable far wrapper for INT 1Ah subfunctions 2–5 (RTC read/set time and date). [bp+6]=AH subfunction; four far byte pointers at [bp+8/c/10/14] are in/out for CH (hours or century), CL (minutes or year), DH (seconds or month), DL (DST flag or day), all BCD. Clears CF before calling. On success (CF clear) writes results back through the pointers and returns AX=0; on error (CF set) skips write-back.

1582:0AD4.label: rtc_read_datetime
1582:0AD4.comment: Reads the full RTC date and time into a caller-supplied struct (far ptr at [bp+6:8]). Calls bios_rtc_call(AH=2) for time (CH=hours, CL=minutes, DH=seconds) and bios_rtc_call(AH=4) for date (CH=century, CL=year, DH=month, DL=day). Converts each BCD field to binary via bcd_to_bin and writes into the struct: [+0]=1 (valid flag), [+2]=century, [+4]=year, [+6]=month, [+8]=day, [+0a]=hours, [+0c]=minutes, [+0e]=seconds. Returns AX=0 on success.

1582:0C4A.label: bin_to_bcd
1582:0C4A.comment: Converts a binary byte in [bp+6] to packed BCD. Divides by 10 twice to extract tens and units digits, then packs them as (tens<<4)|units. Returns the BCD byte in AL. Inverse of bcd_to_bin (1582:0C6F).

1582:0C6F.label: bcd_to_bin
1582:0C6F.comment: Converts a packed BCD byte in [bp+6] to binary. Shifts right 4 to get the tens digit, multiplies by 10, then adds the low nibble (units digit). Returns binary value in AX. Inverse of bin_to_bcd (1582:0C4A).

1582:0D1B.label: cmos_read_reg
1582:0D1B.comment: Reads one raw CMOS/RTC register directly via I/O ports 0x70/0x71. Acquires hardware lock (func_36C5_0744), then writes ([bp+6] | 0x80) to port 0x70 (selects register and disables NMI), reads the result from port 0x71 via io_read_with_delay, then writes 0x0D to port 0x70 to re-enable NMI, and releases the lock (func_36C5_06D0). Returns raw register byte in AX.

1582:1197.label: io_read_with_delay
1582:1197.comment: Busy-waits 40 iterations then reads one byte from the I/O port passed in [bp+6] via func_36C5_0A22 (inp). Returns the byte in AX. The delay gives slow hardware (e.g. CMOS) time to settle after an address write to port 0x70.

1582:11BC.label: io_write_with_delay
1582:11BC.comment: Busy-waits 40 iterations then writes the byte in [bp+8] to the I/O port in [bp+6] via func_36C5_06EE (IO out). The delay gives slow hardware (e.g. CMOS) time to settle before the next access.

1582:1427.label: read_bios_timer_direct
1582:1427.comment: Reads the 32-bit BIOS tick counter directly from memory at 0x0000:0x046C (low word in AX, high word in DX), bypassing INT 1Ah. Does not update the midnight-crossed flag. Used by elapsed_ticks_ge for fast polling.

1582:1445.label: elapsed_ticks_ge
1582:1445.comment: Checks whether at least [bp+0a] ticks have elapsed since the tick value in [bp+6]. Reads the current tick count via read_bios_timer_direct, subtracts the start tick, and compares against the threshold. Returns AX=0 if not yet elapsed (jl taken); the unexecuted gap at 145D–1461 presumably returns nonzero when the threshold is reached.

1D76:0340.label: rtc_test_main
1D76:0340.comment: RTC test main routine. Entry stub calls func_1CF4_02EC for init then far-jumps to 1E88:0000 where the real body lives. Creates a full-screen (mode 0) or windowed (mode 1) dialog. Captures BDA start tick into [bp+0xfe2a/0xfe2c] and sets state 5 threshold [bp-0x16]=0xDA=218 ticks before the outer loop starts. Drives a 7-state machine via a jump table at CS:0x0BA3 (indexed by [bp-0x1a]*2); outer loop ran 4062 times total in emulated-clock trace. State flow for Compare Elapsed Time: states 0-2 set up and verify alarm → state 3 waits for RTC alarm (2179 iterations, timeout=127 ticks from alarm-set at [bp+0xfe2e]) → state 4 immediate transition → state 5 polls elapsed BDA ticks from TEST START against 218-tick threshold (1878+ iterations). Pass counts clamped to 2; results displayed via func_1354 text routines.

36C5:0698.label: dos_get_date
36C5:0698.comment: Reads the current system date via INT 21h AH=2Ah (DOS Get Date). Returns CX=year, DH=month, DL=day, AL=day-of-week. Writes results into the struct at the far ptr arg [bp+06]: [bx+0]=day (DL), [bx+1]=month (DH), [bx+2]=year (CX), [bx+4]=day-of-week (AL). Returns AX=0. Called every outer-loop iteration (2903×) with DS:0xCF4A.

36C5:06B4.label: dos_get_time
36C5:06B4.comment: Reads the current system time via INT 21h AH=2Ch (DOS Get Time). Returns CH=hours, CL=minutes, DH=seconds, DL=centiseconds. Writes results into the struct at the far ptr arg [bp+06]: [bx+0]=hours (CH), [bx+1]=minutes (CL), [bx+2]=seconds (DH), [bx+3]=centiseconds (DL). Returns AX=0. Called every outer-loop iteration (3337× including a second call site at 1E88:0F26) with DS:0xCF74.

1E88:0D61.label: update_datetime_display
1E88:0D61.comment: Delta-updates the live clock display field, re-rendering only changed values. Three near-far-call args (push cs; call near; caller cleans 6 bytes): [bp+06]=force flag (0=delta-only; nonzero path is an untraced 13-byte gap at 0D6F, presumably forces a full redraw); [bp+08]:[bp+0a]=field descriptor far ptr (SS:[bp+0xfe32] from the outer loop). Executes 2903 times per test run but renders only when a value changes (7 times in trace). Three independent change-checks in sequence, each comparing a current value against a 'last displayed' global: (1) RTC seconds [0xCF72] vs [0xCF5E]: if changed, copies hours/minutes/seconds from [0xCF6E:70:72] (from rtc_read_datetime at 0xCF64) into last-displayed globals [0xCF5A:5C:5E], formats via func_1582_0BCC with base struct at DS:0xCF50, then positions cursor at column 0x1F/row 1 via func_31A9_0D98 and writes via write_text_to_field. (2) DOS seconds [0xCF76] vs [0xCF62]: if changed, copies [0xCF74:76] (hours:minutes word and seconds:centiseconds word from dos_get_time struct) into [0xCF60:62], positions cursor at column 0x1F/row 2, formats hours/minutes/seconds using format string at DS:0x820A, renders via func_31A9_0EC0. (3) RTC day [0xCF6C] vs [0xCF58]: if changed, 82-byte gap at 0E40–0E92 (7 executions, untraced detail) presumably re-renders the date line. (4) DOS day [0xCF4A] vs [0xCF44]: if changed, 66-byte gap at 0EA1–0EE3 (untraced). Returns after all checks.

36C5:06D2.label: longjmp
36C5:06D2.comment: C runtime longjmp. Takes two arguments pushed before the far call: ES:[0x0364] (saved IP) and ES:[0x0366] (saved CS) from a setjmp jump buffer. Resets SP to the caller's saved BP (unwinding the stack), overwrites the saved return CS:IP at [bp+0x12]/[bp+0x10] with the jump-buffer values, then pops the full saved register context (es/ds/di/si/bp/bx/bx/dx) and retf's to the saved address. Never returns to its call site (1306:0335 is dead code).

1E88:1174.label: rtc_cancel_or_set_alarm
1E88:1174.comment: First cancels any pending RTC alarm by calling bios_rtc_call(AH=7, scratch×4). If [bp+0a] is nonzero and the datetime struct at [bp+6:8] is valid (struct[0]!=0), reads hours/minutes/seconds from the struct (fields +0a/+0c/+0e), converts each to BCD via bin_to_bcd, then calls bios_rtc_call(AH=6) to program the new alarm. Returns AX=0 on success.

1306:0300.label: int08h_hook
1306:0300.comment: CheckIt's INT 08h (timer IRQ0) hook. Saves all registers (pusha-style: ax/cx/dx/bx/sp/bp/si/di/ds/es), sets DS=0x48A2, increments its own 32-bit internal tick counter at [DS:0x0C7A/0x0C7C] (+1 low word, +carry high), then calls longjmp (36C5:06D2) to chain to the original INT 08h vector stored at [0x0366]:[0x0364] (should be F000:0008, the BIOS handler). Never returns to 1306:0335 — longjmp unwinds the stack and retfs directly to the original BIOS handler, which increments the BDA tick counter at 0x0000:0x046C and sends EOI.

1E88:0844.label: state3_alarm_wait
1E88:0844.comment: State 3 handler: waits for the RTC alarm to fire while checking a BDA-tick elapsed timeout. Each iteration calls func_1354_12E5 (always returns AX=0), then checks [0x8206]: if zero (alarm not yet fired) falls through to elapsed_ticks_ge at 1E88:0898 with start=[bp+0xfe2e] (captured at 1E88:077A just before alarm set) threshold=[bp+0xfe28]=0x7F (127 ticks, ~7 sec timeout); if nonzero (alarm fired) captures display data and transitions to state 4. With real-clock: alarm fired after ~60 iterations (37 BDA ticks total). With emulated-clock: alarm fired after 2179 iterations (184 BDA ticks total); timeout elapsed check ran 2178 times without triggering, confirming alarm fired within the 127-tick window.

1E88:0910.label: state5_elapsed_check
1E88:0910.comment: State 5 handler — polls elapsed BDA ticks from test start. Each iteration calls func_1354_12E5 (always AX=0), then calls elapsed_ticks_ge(start_low=[bp+0xfe2a], start_high=[bp+0xfe2c], threshold=[bp-0x16]=0xDA=218). The start tick is captured at 1E88:027F at the very beginning of the test (before the outer loop), so state 5 measures total elapsed time from test start, not from alarm fire. Threshold of 218 ticks ≈ 12 seconds of emulated time. With real-clock: 37 total BDA ticks, never converged. With emulated-clock (full run): 2902 iterations before jne 0x095A taken once — threshold crossed exactly once. The test needs 2 passes through state 5 success to score 2 for this sub-test, but at PIT divisor ~4533 (263 Hz BDA) one pass consumes ~2902 of the ~5083 total outer-loop iterations, leaving no budget for a second.

1354:06D1.label: render_text_lines_to_field
1354:06D1.comment: Renders newline-delimited text data into a CheckIt UI field in two phases. Variable far-call args (callers clean 6–14 bytes): [bp+06]=flags (bit 2: gate display on CMOS-status [0x0cac]); [bp+08]:[bp+0a]=source data far ptr; [bp+0c] and higher = auxiliary words forwarded to func_36C5_091C. Allocates 162 bytes of locals; 158-byte working buffer at SS:[bp+ff62]. Phase 1 (0x06D1–0x0769): loads ES from [0xb638], checks ES:[0x0aa2] against 2 (9-byte gap 06E4–06ED, untraced: mode-2 path). If source ptr is non-null (3-byte gap 06F5–06F8, untraced: null path), calls func_36C5_091C(SS:buf, src_ptr, SS:&[bp+0c]) to populate the working buffer from the source data; adds strlen(buf) (func_36C5_051C) to running total at ES:[0x0aa8]. Loops: func_36C5_08EE(ptr, '\n'=0x0a) finds the next newline — returned ptr incremented by 1, strlen of next segment added to ES:[0x0aa8], then func_36C5_08EE again — until null (list exhausted). Post-loop: if [0x0cb0]≠0 executes 55-byte gap (0x0770–0x07A7, untraced); if [0x0ca6]==0, jumps ahead 472 bytes to 0x0989, skipping the untraced body. Phase 2 (0x0989–0x0ACF): clears done-flag [bp-0x02]; if bit 2 of flags is set and ES:[0x0cac]≠0, sets [bp-0x02]=1 (44-byte gap 099B–09C7, untraced). Transfers local buffer to global text buffer 0x40af:0x05fe via func_36C5_041A. Display loop (lbl_09EB): calls func_36C5_08EE(0x40af:0x05fe, '\n') to find next newline; null → exits. Null-terminates segment at that position (ES:[BX]=0); appends/concatenates via func_36C5_04E6 and func_36C5_041A with separator from DS:0x0d60; calls func_31A9_067C(SS:local_buf, ES:[0x0ab6], [0x0caa]:[0x0ca8]) to write the line into the field at the current row index (ES:[0x0ab6] loaded from [0xb63c]). AX≠1 path has 38-byte gap (0A8D–0AB3, untraced). Loops while ES:[0x0ab6]≠0xffff; exits at 0xACF.

16E4:0698.label: render_right_aligned_value_in_field
16E4:0698.comment: Writes a source string right-aligned into a display field sub-object, then refreshes the field frame. Five far-call args (caller cleans 0xA bytes): [bp+06]:[bp+08]=outer field descriptor far ptr, [bp+0a]:[bp+0c]=source string far ptr, [bp+0e]=row cursor threshold. Step 1: if outer_desc[+0x32] != 0, calls func_31A9_0072(outer_desc+0x10) to reset the embedded text sub-field before writing. Step 2: calls write_aligned_text(row=1, align=0xFFFE=right-align, src=[bp+0a:0c], dest=outer_desc+0x10) — right-aligns the source string at row 1 within the sub-field embedded at descriptor+0x10. Step 3: compares outer_desc[+0x0c] (row cursor) against [bp+0x0e]; 4-byte gap at 16E4:06E3–06E7 handles the cursor-below-threshold case (never executed in trace: cursor=1 ≥ threshold=1, so jge taken). Step 4: calls func_16E4_0597 with the outer descriptor to redraw the field border/frame. Returns AX=0. Called once from state5_success_path (1E88:0A6F) after the FPU elapsed-time comparison passes, with the formatted elapsed-time string (built by display_elapsed_time + func_36C5_041A at SS:[bp+0xfefa]) and the measurement results field descriptor at SS:[bp+0xfe78].

1354:175C.label: display_test_result
1354:175C.comment: Selects a result string from a far-pointer table and renders it into a field. Three far-call args: [bp+06]=result_index (1=render result, other=gap path never executed), [bp+08]:[bp+0a]=field descriptor far ptr. Checks DS:[0x0c9c]: if 0 (our path) → table_index=[bp-0x02]=6; gap at 1354:1770 (5 bytes, never executed) presumably copies [0x0c9c] into [bp-0x02] when nonzero. When result_index==1: loads ES from global segment ptr at [0xb62c], computes bx=table_index*4, pushes the far ptr at ES:[bx+0x0646] (offset) and ES:[bx+0x0648] (segment) — selecting entry 6 (at ES:[0x065E]/[0x0660]) in execution — as the result string, pushes the field descriptor, calls render_result_in_field (16E4:02F6), cleans 8 bytes, then calls func_16E4_1429(1) for post-render finalization. Gap at 1354:179D-17BD (32 bytes, never executed) handles result_index!=1, then reaches the same add-sp/finalization tail. Called from 1E88:0BDB at the state-5 success conclusion to show the final RTC test result string in the display field.

16E4:018E.label: draw_field_rule
16E4:018E.comment: Draws a full-width horizontal rule (CP437 0xC4 '─') across a field at a given row. Four near-far-call args (push cs; call near; caller cleans 8 bytes): [bp+06]=row within field, [bp+08]=column offset to write into descriptor's column cursor [bx+0x10], [bp+0a]:[bp+0c]=field descriptor far ptr. Saves [bx+0x10] → [bp-0x02]. Sets [bx+0x10]=[bp+0x08]. Calls func_31A9_0D98(descriptor, col=0, row=[bp+0x06]) to position cursor. Calls func_31A9_0F84(descriptor, char=0xFFC4, width=[bx+0x0a]-[bx+0x08]+1) to paint the horizontal line across the full field width (0xC4=─ in CP437, 0xFF=attribute). Restores [bx+0x10] from [bp-0x02].

16E4:025F.label: render_value_at_column
16E4:025F.comment: Renders a static label string and a centered value string into a field, at a given column, then restores field state. Six near-far-call args (push cs; call near; caller cleans 12 bytes): [bp+06]=row (passed directly to write_aligned_text as the row arg; caller computes this from field box dimensions [bx+0x06]-[bx+0x04]), [bp+08]=absolute column to temporarily set in the field descriptor's column cursor ([bx+0x10]), [bp+0a]:[bp+0c]=value string far ptr, [bp+0e]:[bp+10]=field descriptor far ptr. Saves original column [bx+0x10] → [bp-0x04]. Captures bit1 of [bx+0x14] (trailing-space-pad disable flag): uses sbb cx,cx+neg cx trick so [bp-0x02]=1 if bit1 was clear, 0 if set. Sets [bx+0x10]=[bp+0x08] and clears bit1 of [bx+0x14] (enabling trailing-space padding for both writes). First call: write_aligned_text(descriptor, string=ES:[0x2034] (ES loaded from global at [0xb6aa]), align=left/0, row) — writes a fixed label/spacer string. Second call: write_aligned_text(descriptor, string=[bp+0a]:[bp+0c], align=center/0xFFFF, row) — writes the value centered. Restores [bx+0x10] from [bp-0x04]. Restores bit1 of [bx+0x14]: if [bp-0x02]==1 (bit1 was clear), clears it again (no-op effectively); gap at 16E4:02E5-02EE (9 bytes, never executed in trace) restores bit1 to set when it was originally set. Caller func_16E4_02F6 passes column=0x002b (43).

16E4:02F6.label: render_result_in_field
16E4:02F6.comment: Renders a test result value into a field with a horizontal separator rule above it. Four far-call args (caller cleans 8 bytes via 'add sp, 0x0008'): [bp+06]:[bp+08]=value string far ptr, [bp+0a]:[bp+0c]=field descriptor far ptr. Loads the descriptor to compute height=[bx+0x06]-[bx+0x04] (bottom minus top dimension). Makes two calls: (1) draw_field_rule(descriptor, col_start=1, row=height-1) — paints a full-width '─' rule at the penultimate row of the field. (2) render_value_at_column(descriptor, string=[bp+06]:[bp+08], col=43, row=height) — writes the value string centered at column 43 on the last row. The result is a horizontal rule followed immediately by the centered value text on the line below it, used to display CheckIt test result strings (e.g. pass/fail) within a display field.

31A9:0358.label: write_aligned_text
31A9:0358.comment: Writes a string into a UI field with configurable horizontal alignment. Six far-call args (caller cleans 12 bytes via 'add sp, 0x000c'): [bp+06]=row within the field (passed to func_31A9_0D98 as the cursor row; callers use 0 or 1), [bp+08]=alignment sentinel (0=left, 0xFFFF=center, 0xFFFE=right-align, any other value=absolute column offset), [bp+0a]:[bp+0c]=source string far ptr, [bp+0e]:[bp+10]=field descriptor far ptr. Acquires display lock on CS:0xC6C0 via func_31A9_129C. Null-checks the string ptr ([bp+0a]|[bp+0c]==0 → skip via gap at 31A9:037E). Calls func_36C5_051C to get string length → [bp-0x08]. Computes field width as descriptor[bx+0x0a]-descriptor[bx+0x08]+1 → [bp-0x02]. Alignment dispatch on [bp+0x08]: 0xFFFF → center column = (field_width - strlen)/2 (via cwd+sub+sar); 0xFFFE → right column = field_width - strlen; 0x0000 → column stays 0 (left); other → use [bp+0x08] as absolute column. Clamps column to ≥0 (gap at 31A9:03DE). Calls func_31A9_0D98(descriptor, column, row) to position the field cursor, then write_text_to_field (31A9:10D4) to render the string. Releases lock via func_31A9_12C6. Returns dx:ax from write_text_to_field.

31A9:10D4.label: write_text_to_field
31A9:10D4.comment: Drives a render loop to write a string into a UI field descriptor. Four far-call args (caller cleans 8 bytes via 'add sp, 0x0008'): [bp+06]:[bp+08]=source string far ptr (walking pointer, updated per chunk), [bp+0a]:[bp+0c]=field descriptor far ptr. Acquires a display lock by calling func_31A9_129C on a buffer at CS:0xC752. Calls func_31A9_451C to validate the field descriptor; if not active (AX=0) skips to epilogue. Main loop (lbl_31A9_1139): calls func_31A9_451C again to get field state via ES:BX, computes remaining column width as [bx+0x0a]-[bx+0x08]-[bx+0x0e]+1, calls render_field_text (31A9:4640) with the current string ptr and width, stores the returned next-string-ptr back into [bp+06]:[bp+08], and loops back via lbl_31A9_111A as long as the return is nonzero (more string to render). Exits the loop when render_field_text returns 0 (string fully consumed). Releases the lock via func_31A9_12C6 and returns dx:ax=0. Gap at 31A9:1109-111A (17 bytes) handles the path when func_31A9_451C returns 0 on the first check (descriptor not ready). Gap at 31A9:1120-1138 (25 bytes) executes only when [bp-0x08]>0 (set by some path not seen in execution trace).

31A9:4640.label: render_field_text
31A9:4640.comment: Renders a fixed-width text field to the CheckIt screen. Five far-call args (caller cleans 10 bytes via 'add sp, 0x000a'): [bp+06]:[bp+08]=source string far ptr, [bp+0a]=max field width (char count), [bp+0c]:[bp+0e]=far ptr to field descriptor struct. Iterates source string up to max_width chars: NUL terminates early (sets end-of-string flag [bp-0x18]=1), TAB (0x09) and LF (0x0A) are handled in gap code, regular chars are stored into a local char buffer at [ES:bx+0x02aa] and a char count [bp-0x2c] is incremented. When char count reaches max_width, sets truncation flag [bp-0x20]=1 and terminates the loop. After the loop calls func_31A9_4E26 to compute the display position for the field, func_31A9_4D18 to compute a second video offset (for the pad region), then looks up the color/attribute from a table at [0xa58c] indexed by the field-type word at struct+0x16 (adding 1 to AH for the main text attribute). Calls video_write_chars (31A9:4EDE) twice: first for the accumulated chars with the field color, then (if bit 1 of struct+0x14 flags is clear) for trailing-space padding with a dimmer attribute (AH+2). Updates the field column cursor: adds char count to struct+0x0e. If truncation flag is set (string exactly filled the field), increments the row counter at struct+0x0c and resets struct+0x0e to 0. Returns dx:ax = pointer to the rendered region.

31A9:4EDE.label: video_write_chars
31A9:4EDE.comment: Writes CX words from source buffer (DS:SI) to destination buffer (ES:DI), typically VGA text-mode video memory (0xB800). Six far-call args, caller cleans 6 words via 'add sp, 0x0c': [bp+06]=control word (BH=write mode, AL:AH=char:attr or fill value), [bp+08]=count, [bp+0a]=source offset→SI, [bp+0c]=source segment→DS, [bp+0e]=dest offset→DI, [bp+10]=dest segment→ES. Reads CheckIt global display flags: ES:[0x0138] via segment ptr at [0xcae4] (sub-mode; ja 4F0C skips gap when nonzero), then DH=ES:[0x012a] via ptr at [0xcae0] (text/graphics flag; DH=0=text mode, DH≠0=graphics path in gap 4F4C–4F9B, never executed). Write dispatch on BH: BH=2 → rep stosw (fill entire dest with AX from first source word); BH≠2,≠0 → word-by-word: stosw then reload only AL=[SI] each iteration (attribute AH stays fixed from the first word); BH=0 → gap at 4FB5 (never executed). After write, restores ES from [0xcae6], checks [0x0121]==1 for a post-write update (gap 4FC5–4FD2 never executed). Count=0 → immediate return via jmp 4FD2.

1E88:095A.label: state5_success_path
1E88:095A.comment: State 5 success path — reached when elapsed_ticks_ge returns non-zero (BDA elapsed >= 218 ticks). Executed exactly ONCE in the full test run (after 2902 state-5 iterations). Calls func_1306_000A (push 0x000F arg) and func_1306_008B, then performs FPU comparison of actual elapsed time against expected bounds (jae 0x0A26 taken = pass, gap at 0A23 = fail path, never executed). Records result via func_16E4_0698, increments pass counter [0x8208] at 1E88:0A77 (from 0 to 1), then transitions to state 6 (mov [bp-0x1a], 0x0006 at 1E88:0B9B). Sub-test needs 2 increments for a full score of 2; only 1 was accumulated.

1E88:120E.label: display_elapsed_time
1E88:120E.comment: Converts a 64-bit float RTC measurement into integer time components and renders them to a display field. Six args (push cs; call near; caller cleans 0x0c): [bp+06]=field descriptor offset, [bp+08]=field descriptor segment (SS), [bp+0a..0x10]=four words of a 64-bit IEEE double holding the measured elapsed time. Passes the four data words to func_36C5_1E25 to dereference/retrieve the underlying value, copies the resulting 8 bytes into a local qword at [bp-0x18] via 4×movsw. FPU: loads that qword, adds constant at [0xbd0a], multiplies by constant at [0xbcfa] (affine scale to normalize the float to integer ticks), then calls func_36C5_0178 to convert the FPU result to a 32-bit integer stored at [bp-0x08:-0x06]. Decomposes via successive long-division (func_36C5_160E = quotient, func_36C5_0720 = remainder update in-place): ÷100 → [bp-0a]=coarse unit (e.g. total seconds or primary display value), remainder in [bp-8]; ÷60 → [bp-0c]=first sub-component, remainder in [bp-8]; ÷60 → [bp-0e]=second sub-component, remainder in [bp-8]; then [bp-10]=[bp-8] (copies low word). Renders a header line into the field via func_36C5_04E6 with format string at DS:0x8228. Then loops i=0..2 over [bp-0x10], [bp-0x0e], [bp-0x0c] (highest to lowest order): a [bp-4] flag suppresses display of leading-zero components (set=suppress, cleared on first nonzero); nonzero values are formatted with string DS:0x822a and rendered via func_16E4_0098 + func_36C5_0460. After the loop, always renders [bp-0a] using format string DS:0x8233 via the same pair. Called from four sites in 1E88 (0x035D, 0x04BF, 0x0A3D, 0x0B7E), always with a 64-bit elapsed-time float and field descriptor at SS:[bp+0xfefa].

1E88:0292.label: poll_loop_top
1E88:0292.comment: Outer polling loop body (2903 iterations). Each pass: (1) reads RTC date+time via rtc_read_datetime into DS:0xCF64; (2) reads DOS system time (INT 21h/2Ch) into DS:0xCF74; (3) reads DOS system date (INT 21h/2Ah) into DS:0xCF4A; (4) calls update_datetime_display to re-render changed clock fields; (5) loads state [bp-0x1a] — if > 6 loops back, else dispatches via 7-entry jump table at CS:0x0BA3.

lineComments.12E0:0163 72 05: CF set = INT 1Ah error (clock not operating); skip write-back
lineComments.1582:0D29 0C 80: set bit 7 to disable NMI during CMOS access (standard protocol)
lineComments.1582:0D47 B8 0D 00: write 0x0D to port 0x70: re-enables NMI (bit 7 clear) and selects a safe register
lineComments.1582:1432 C7 46 FC 6C 04: far pointer offset 0x046C = BIOS tick counter low word at 0x0000:0x046C
lineComments.1E88:0033 75 12: ES:[0x01a2] is test mode: 0=full-screen 80x25 dialog, nonzero=44x18 windowed dialog at row 6 col 3
lineComments.1E88:027A 9A 27 14 82 15: capture BDA start tick into [bp+0xfe2a/0xfe2c] — state 5 measures elapsed ticks from this point
lineComments.1E88:0287 C7 86 28 FE 7F 00: state 3 elapsed timeout threshold = 0x7F = 127 BDA ticks (~7 sec); if alarm hasn't fired within this many ticks from [bp+0xfe2e], give up
lineComments.1E88:028D C7 46 EA DA 00: state 5 tick threshold = 0xDA = 218 BDA ticks (~12 sec from test start); elapsed_ticks_ge must return non-zero for test to pass
lineComments.1E88:02CF 77 C1: state > 6: no matching handler, continue polling loop
lineComments.1E88:0369 DC 16 C8 BC: FPU: compare loaded datetime (as float) against upper bound at [0xbcc8]
lineComments.1E88:0384 DC 16 D0 BC: FPU: compare loaded datetime (as float) against lower bound at [0xbcd0]
lineComments.1E88:044F 7E 03: clamp pass/fail result count: if ax <= 2 keep it, else force ax=2
lineComments.1E88:0292 B8 64 CF: loop top (2903×): read RTC date+time into struct at DS:0xCF64 via rtc_read_datetime
lineComments.1E88:02A4 9A B4 06 C5 36: read DOS system time (INT 21h/2Ch) into struct at DS:0xCF74: hours/minutes/seconds/centiseconds
lineComments.1E88:02B1 9A 98 06 C5 36: read DOS system date (INT 21h/2Ah) into struct at DS:0xCF4A: day/month/year/day-of-week
lineComments.1E88:02B9 8D 86 32 FE: SS:[bp+0xFE32] = live clock display field descriptor
lineComments.1E88:02C3 E8 9B 0A: delta-update clock display: re-render RTC time, DOS time, and date fields only when changed
lineComments.1E88:0528 B8 64 00: expected alarm ticks = minutes*100 + seconds; compare against [0xcf4c]
lineComments.1E88:118E B8 07 00: INT 1Ah AH=7: cancel RTC alarm; all four output pointers point to scratch
lineComments.1E88:11F8 B8 06 00: INT 1Ah AH=6: set RTC alarm to CH=hours, CL=minutes, DH=seconds (BCD)
lineComments.1306:0316 26 83 06 7A 0C 01: increment CheckIt's own internal 32-bit tick counter low word at [DS:0x0C7A]
lineComments.1306:031C 26 83 16 7C 0C 00: carry into high word of CheckIt's internal tick counter at [DS:0x0C7C]
lineComments.1306:0330 9A D2 06 C5 36: chain to original INT 08h via longjmp (36C5:06D2); never returns here — stack is unwound and retf jumps to F000:0008 (BIOS timer handler)
lineComments.1E88:0775 9A 27 14 82 15: capture state 3 timeout start tick into [bp+0xfe2e/0xfe30] — immediately before alarm is programmed via rtc_cancel_or_set_alarm
lineComments.1E88:07E6 E8 8B 09: program RTC alarm: call rtc_cancel_or_set_alarm with datetime struct at [bp-0x3e] (copied from [0xcf64]); [0x8206] cleared to 0 at 07D6 just before this
lineComments.1E88:0871 83 3E 06 82 00: [0x8206]: RTC alarm-fired flag set by INT 4Ah handler at 1E88:0FB2. 0 = alarm not yet fired (fall through to elapsed check); nonzero = alarm has fired (take state-4 transition path)
lineComments.1E88:0876 74 14: alarm not yet fired → check state 3 elapsed timeout (127-tick limit from [bp+0xfe2e]); when alarm fires this falls through instead
lineComments.1E88:0898 9A 45 14 82 15: state 3 elapsed timeout check: elapsed_ticks_ge(start=[bp+0xfe2e], threshold=0x7F=127). With emulated-clock ran 2178 times without triggering — alarm always fired before timeout
lineComments.1E88:0FB2 C7 06 06 82 01 00: INT 4Ah (RTC alarm) handler: sets alarm-fired flag [0x8206]=1; installed at 1E88:07CE via func_36C5_0682 with handler address 1E88:0FA2
lineComments.1E88:083C C7 46 E6 03 00: state = 3: enter alarm-wait / elapsed-ticks polling loop
lineComments.1E88:08D0 C7 46 E6 04 00: state = 4: alarm has fired; immediate transition to state 5
lineComments.1E88:0908 C7 46 E6 05 00: state = 5: begin Compare Elapsed Time check against BDA tick counter
lineComments.1E88:0940 FF 76 EA: push threshold ([bp-0x16]=218) for elapsed_ticks_ge — total BDA ticks required since test start (~12 sec at 18.2 Hz)
lineComments.1E88:0943 FF B6 2C FE: push start tick high word ([bp+0xfe2c]) for elapsed_ticks_ge — captured at test init (1E88:027F), not at alarm set
lineComments.1E88:0947 FF B6 2A FE: push start tick low word ([bp+0xfe2a]) for elapsed_ticks_ge — BDA counter value captured at very beginning of test
lineComments.1582:144F 2B 46 06: elapsed_low = current_low - start_low; only 16-bit subtraction, ignores high word wraparound
lineComments.1582:145B 7C 05: jl: elapsed < threshold (signed 16-bit) → return AX=0; fall-through to gap at 145D–1461 returns AX≠0 (confirmed: mov ax,1 / jmp 0x7ff6 in decompressed dump)
lineComments.1E88:0955 75 03: jne 0x095A: state 5 elapsed check — taken once (BDA ticks >= 218 reached after 2902 iterations); fell through to jmp 0x0292 2901 times
lineComments.1E88:0A77 FF 06 08 82: inc [0x8208]: Compare Elapsed Time pass counter; incremented from 0 to 1 (sub-test needs 2 for full score of 2, but at 263 Hz BDA only one pass fits within the outer-loop budget)
lineComments.1E88:0B9B C7 46 E6 06 00: state = 6: final state after state5_success_path completes; outer loop returns to 0292 and then exits the test
lineComments.1E88:05E4 26 83 BF B1 02 0C: cumulative result check: cmp [bx+0x02b1], 0x000C — tests whether all 6 sub-tests accumulated their maximum score (6 × 2 = 12). ES:[bx+0x02b1] holds the running total. je NOT taken → fail path; test reports failure because only 11 (or fewer) was accumulated — Compare Elapsed Time scored 1/2 instead of 2/2
lineComments.1E88:05EA 74 03: je 0x05ef: would jump to 201-byte pass path (05EF–06B8) if cumulative score == 12; NOT taken (score < 12) → fall through to jmp 06B8
lineComments.1E88:05EC E9 C9 00: jmp 0x06B8: unconditional fail path taken because cumulative score < 12; 201-byte gap at 05EF–06B8 (the overall-pass path) remains unexecuted
lineComments.31A9:4EE5 8E 06 E4 CA: mov es, [0xcae4]: load CheckIt data segment selector into ES to read global display-mode flags
lineComments.31A9:4EE9 26 A1 38 01: mov ax, es:[0x0138]: read sub-mode flag; 0 = take gap path at 4EF2, nonzero = skip straight to 4F0C (always nonzero in trace)
lineComments.31A9:4EF0 77 1A: ja 0x4f0c: skip 26-byte gap (4EF2–4F0C) when [0x0138] > 0; gap path (never executed) likely handles a special display sub-mode init
lineComments.31A9:4F0C 8E 06 E0 CA: mov es, [0xcae0]: switch ES to a second CheckIt data segment to read text/graphics mode flag
lineComments.31A9:4F10 26 8A 36 2A 01: mov dh, es:[0x012a]: DH = text/graphics mode flag; 0 = text mode (executes write loop), nonzero = graphics mode (gap at 4F4C–4F9B, never executed)
lineComments.31A9:4F18 8E 5E 0C: mov ds, [bp+0x0c]: source buffer segment → DS
lineComments.31A9:4F1E 8E 46 10: mov es, [bp+0x10]: destination buffer segment → ES (typically 0xB800 for VGA text memory)
lineComments.31A9:4F29 E9 A6 00: jmp 0x4fd2: count == 0, nothing to write — skip straight to epilogue
lineComments.31A9:4F2C 8C C0: mov ax, es: AX = dest segment (from [bp+0x10]); compare against source segment to detect same-segment overlap (gap at 4F33–4F37 handles that case, never executed)
lineComments.31A9:4F44 80 FE 00: cmp dh, 0x00: branch on text vs graphics mode; DH=0 (text) → je 4F9B (write loop); DH≠0 (graphics) → fall to 79-byte gap at 4F4C–4F9B (never executed)
lineComments.31A9:4F47 8B 5E 06: mov bx, [bp+0x06]: load control word; BH = write mode (2=fill, 0=skip, other=copy), BL = attribute or fill char
lineComments.31A9:4F9B 8B 04: mov ax, [si]: load first source word (AL=char, AH=attribute) — used as fill value for BH=2, or first word written for BH=other
lineComments.31A9:4F9D 80 FF 02: cmp bh, 0x02: BH=2 → rep stosw fill mode (write AX to entire dest range)
lineComments.31A9:4FA2 80 FF 00: cmp bh, 0x00: BH=0 → gap at 4FB5 (never executed); otherwise fall through to word-by-word copy loop
lineComments.31A9:4FA7 AB: stosw: write AX (char+attr) to ES:DI, advance DI by 2
lineComments.31A9:4FA8 83 C6 02: add si, 0x0002: advance source pointer past the word just written
lineComments.31A9:4FAB 8A 04: mov al, [si]: reload only the character byte (AL) for next iteration; AH (attribute) stays fixed from the first source word — all chars share one attribute
lineComments.31A9:4FB1 F3 AB: rep stosw: fill mode — write AX into ES:DI CX times (paint a uniform char+attr across the destination range)
lineComments.31A9:4FB8 8E 06 E6 CA: mov es, [0xcae6]: restore ES to a third CheckIt data segment to check post-write update flag
lineComments.31A9:4FBC 26 A1 21 01: mov ax, es:[0x0121]: read post-write notification flag; 1 = trigger display refresh (gap 4FC5–4FD2, never executed)
